title: karpatiosorbus paxiana
intro: Karpatiosorbus paxiana, also known as the Pax's service tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to the Eastern Carpathian Mountains, with a history of traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its medicinal value. Karpatiosorbus paxiana is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues.
